input: {"questions":[{"question":"How do you want to drive the next phase?","header":"Next phase","options":[{"label":"Answer the 8 gaps, then /plan","description":"I walk you through the 8 unanswered questions from the hackathon brief one batch at a time, then we run /plan with full context. Highest discipline, ~10-15 min of Q&A before any architecture happens."},{"label":"Sketch movement model first, then gaps","description":"Question 5 (the 'alive' feel вҖ” wandering / schooling / weather-reactive) is the demo's soul. We sketch 2-3 concrete movement models with the art kit in mind, you pick one, then we answer remaining gaps with that anchor."},{"label":"Frame demo flow first (judge POV)","description":"Question 7 (in N seconds, what does a judge get?) anchors everything else. We write the 30-second elevator pitch the visual must deliver, then back-fill the other gaps from there."},{"label":"Just /plan now, decide as we go","description":"Skip Q&A, run /plan with current context and let the discovery interview surface gaps. Faster start but PRD will have more 'TBD' until we decide later."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Which movement model anchors the observatory's 'alive' feel?","header":"Movement","options":[{"label":"Wuxing Currents (flow-field)","description":"Invisible vector field over the tsuheji map, modulated by current cosmic+IRL weather. Each puruhani drifts along the current matching its primary element; when fire weather surges, fire-affinity entities accelerate. On-chain actions emit a ripple impulse. Scales trivially to thousands; emergent schooling without per-entity AI. Reads as 'ocean of petals' вҖ” hypnotic, ambient, breath-aligned. Tradeoff: less per-character intimacy.","preview":"  WUXING CURRENTS
  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

   в•ӯв”Җtsuheji-mapв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•®
   в”Ӯ  вҶҳ  вҶҳ   вҶ“   вҶҷ   вҶҷ       в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ В·вҖўВ·  В·вҖўВ· В·вҖўВ· В·вҖўВ·  В·вҖўВ·   в”Ӯ  вҶҗ entities drift
   в”Ӯ  вҶ“   вҶ“ в—үвҶҳ  вҶ“   вҶҳ   вҶ“    в”Ӯ    along element
   в”Ӯ В·вҖўВ· В·вҖўВ·  В·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ· В·вҖўВ·    в”Ӯ    currents
   в”Ӯ  вҶҳ   вҶҳ   вҶҳ  вҶҳ    вҶҳ      в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  В·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·    в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ woodвҶ’  fireвҶ’  waterвҶ’    в”Ӯ
   в•°в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ҝ

   weather shift = field rotates
   on-chain mint  = в—ү ripple at actor
   breathing hue  = element rhythm pulse

  [O(1) per entity В· 5k+ sprites trivial]"},{"label":"Element Districts (gravitational)","description":"Map loosely partitioned into 5 wuxing biomes woven into tsuheji geography. Puruhani gravitate toward their primary element's district but wander; mixed-affinity wallets are the 'interesting' drifters between zones. Weather shifts gravitational pull вҖ” rainstorm expands water district, fire entities flee. Most legible at a glance for judges. Tradeoff: feels closer to a population map than a living flow.","preview":"  ELEMENT DISTRICTS
  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

   в•ӯв”Җtsuheji-mapв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•®
   в”Ӯ в•ӯWOODв•®     в•ӯв”ҖFIREв”Җв”Җв•®    в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ в”ӮВ·вҖўВ·вҖўв”Ӯ     в”ӮВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖў в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖўВ·вҖўв”Ӯ   вҶ— в”Ӯ вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖў в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ  вҶҗ high-affinity
   в”Ӯ в•°в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ҝ  вҶ—  в•°в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ҝ    в”Ӯ    orbits tight
   в”Ӯ      вҶ—вҖўВ·       в—үВ·вҖў      в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ в•ӯEARTHв•®  вҶҳ в•ӯв”ҖMETALв”Җв•®    в”Ӯ  вҶҗ drifters
   в”Ӯ в”ӮВ·вҖўВ·вҖў в”Ӯ   вҶҳв”ӮВ·вҖўВ·вҖў В· в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ    cross zones
   в”Ӯ в•°в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ҝ    в•°в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ҝ    в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ      в•ӯWATERв”Җв•®           в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ      в”ӮВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖўв”Ӯ           в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ      в•°в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ҝ           в”Ӯ
   в•°в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ҝ

  weather shift = districts breathe larger/smaller
  judge legibility: instant вҖ” 'oh, distribution!'"},{"label":"Kanji Flock (wuxing boids)","description":"Classical flocking (separation/alignment/cohesion) but with the wuxing destructive/constructive cycle baked in: same-element entities cohere into schools, clashing pairs (waterвҶ’fire, fireвҶ’metal, etc.) repel. Weather shifts rule weights. On-chain actions = an entity briefly breaks formation to animate a custom keyframe (mint = honey-burst). Most kinetic and 'alive,' best showcase of the breath-rhythm system. Tradeoff: can read as generic boids if styling isn't tight.","preview":"  KANJI FLOCK
  в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ

   в•ӯв”Җtsuheji-mapв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•®
   в”Ӯ      В·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖў              в”Ӯ  вҶҗ fire school
   в”Ӯ     В·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖў                 cohere
   в”Ӯ      В·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖў              в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ   в•і вҶҗ repel               в”Ӯ  вҶҗ water + fire
   в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ    repel (wuxing
   в”Ӯ            В·вҖўВ·вҖў           в”Ӯ    clash)
   в”Ӯ           В·вҖўВ·вҖўВ·вҖў          в”Ӯ  вҶҗ water school
   в”Ӯ            В·вҖўВ·вҖў           в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ   в—ү вҶҗ mint event          в”Ӯ  вҶҗ briefly leaves
   в”Ӯ      в•°honey-burst         в”Ӯ    formation
   в•°в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв•Ҝ

  same-element  вҶ’ cohere
  wuxing clash  вҶ’ repel
  weather       вҶ’ reweights rules
  on-chain act  вҶ’ keyframe break-out"}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Which action vocabulary do we commit to for v0?","header":"Actions","options":[{"label":"Tight 3 вҖ” mint, attack, gift","description":"Smallest viable set: mint = sprite spawn / honey-burst keyframe, attack = sprite slides along inner-star (destruction) edge toward target, gift = sprite slides along pentagon (generation) edge. Three distinct visual grammars. Easiest to make each one feel polished in 4 days."},{"label":"Balanced 5 вҖ” mint, attack, gift, vote, transcend","description":"The brief's 5-action set, swapping in 'transcend' (player ascends to the center of the pentagram momentarily) for 'transfer' to give us a center-stage moment. 5 distinct keyframes вҖ” more variety, more risk that some feel less polished."},{"label":"Wuxing-pure 5 вҖ” one per element-relationship","description":"Each action is named after a wuxing relationship: feed (з”ҹ), clash (е…Ӣ), shelter, drift, ascend. Most brand-aligned vocabulary; the actions read as cosmology rather than crypto verbs. Tradeoff: judges may need a half-second to translate."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Where does the player perspective come from?","header":"POV","options":[{"label":"Pure observatory вҖ” no 'me'","description":"The viewer is a god. No wallet connect, no 'your puruhani.' Camera roams. The vibe is ambient and detached вҖ” you're watching a world breathe. Simplest scope; matches the brief's 'pure simulation' fork."},{"label":"Observatory + focus card","description":"Mostly god's-eye, but clicking any sprite opens the right-hand focus card (mocked wallet, mocked profile from the deterministic adapter). No connect button вҖ” anyone can inspect anyone. Adds an interactive layer without auth complexity."},{"label":"Observatory + Phantom connect","description":"Connect Phantom вҶ’ your wallet's seeded mock profile becomes 'you,' camera nudges to your sprite, your actions can be triggered manually (test buttons). More demoable; opens the auth/wallet rabbit hole the brief flagged as deferred."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"This is a brownfield project with no codebase reality files. How would you like to proceed?","header":"Grounding","options":[{"label":"Skip grounding (Recommended)","description":"Proceed without /ride. Defensible here because: (a) the codebase is the freshly-scaffolded kit + 60-line hackathon brief that already captures it, (b) I explored the full surface this session, (c) hackathon clock is 4 days. Saves 5-15 min."},{"label":"Run /ride","description":"Standard codebase analysis вҖ” produces extracted-prd.md, extracted-sdd.md, component-inventory.md, consistency-report.md. Adds ~5-10 minutes; output is largely redundant with what we already have but gives clean reality citations for the PRD."},{"label":"Run /ride --enriched","description":"Full analysis with gap tracking, decision archaeology, terminology extraction. Adds ~10-15 minutes. Most thorough; overkill for a freshly-scaffolded kit."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Ready to generate the PRD (evolve /ride's draft with the resolved decisions)?","header":"Generate","options":[{"label":"Generate (Recommended)","description":"Edit grimoires/loa/prd.md: rewrite F4.7 movement model from ASSUMPTION to GROUNDED, clean В§7 Open Questions (only Q-pixi remains), add Q6 coordination boundary, add v0.1вҶ’v0.4 iteration ladder, update grounding-summary counts. Conservative edits, preserves /ride's structure."},{"label":"Adjust first","description":"Tell me what to change вҖ” e.g., a decision I have wrong, a phase I should re-interview, or a section that should be larger/smaller."},{"label":"Stop here","description":"Save progress вҖ” we have a usable PRD already from /ride. Resume later with /plan or jump straight to /architect. Not what you expected? /feedback helps us fix it."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Anything to steer before architecture?","header":"Review","options":[{"label":"Continue (Recommended)","description":"Run /architect now to design the system architecture (SDD). Then /sprint-plan, then /simstim for the accelerated build cycle, then /run-bridge for iterative review вҖ” the chain you queued."},{"label":"Adjust","description":"Tell me what to change вҖ” I'll regenerate the PRD. Use this if a decision above is wrong, a missing requirement, or scope is off."},{"label":"Stop here","description":"Save progress вҖ” PRD is ready. Resume later with /architect when you want to continue. Not what you expected? /feedback helps us fix it."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How do you want to handle the run-mode prerequisites?","header":"Setup","options":[{"label":"Set it all up + run autonomous (Recommended)","description":"Commit grimoires/ planning baseline on main, branch off to feature/observatory-v0, set run_mode.enabled: true in .loa.config.yaml, then /run sprint-1 autonomously. End state: draft PR with v0.1 idle frame implemented. Iterate from PR review + /run-bridge."},{"label":"Run sprint-1 only (smaller scope)","description":"Same setup, but invoke /run sprint-1 instead of /run sprint-plan. Single sprint, single PR. Faster than the full plan; same autonomous behavior within Sprint 1."},{"label":"Skip framework, build informally","description":"Acknowledge the framework's autonomous flow doesn't match your iterate-live desire for this hackathon. I'd violate the NEVER rule by /implement-direct. Explicit override needed вҖ” you'd be telling me to bypass review/audit gates for velocity. Risky, but legitimate hackathon call."},{"label":"Pause and rethink","description":"Stop the cycle here. PRD + SDD + sprint plan are saved. We discuss what 'iterate live' should actually look like in this framework before committing to a build approach."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"What's next?","header":"Next move","options":[{"label":"Pause вҖ” you go look at it (Recommended)","description":"Run `pnpm dev`, open /observatory, react. Iterate decisions from what you actually see. The /run loop can resume any time вҖ” commit is on feature/observatory-v0; /review-sprint + /audit-sprint + PR + /run-bridge all still available as separate calls."},{"label":"Continue the autonomous loop","description":"Run /review-sprint sprint-1 next, then /audit-sprint, then draft PR, then /run-bridge. Catches anything the implementation missed before you look. Adds 5-15 min of runtime; produces a more polished landing."},{"label":"Skip review/audit, jump to PR + /run-bridge","description":"Push the branch, open draft PR now, run /run-bridge against the open PR for adversarial review. Faster than full review/audit cycle; trades some local-loop discipline for external-eyes feedback."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How should we ship the 5 dig-validated changes?","header":"Workflow","options":[{"label":"Ad-hoc iteration (matches recent commits) (Recommended)","description":"One logical change per commit, no sprint formalism, no review/audit gates. Matches your last 12 observatory commits. Fastest path on the 3-day hackathon clock; you watch the dev server and validate visually after each commit. Tradeoff: bypasses Loa's review+audit, which is fine for FE polish but means no programmatic correctness gate."},{"label":"Sprint-2 formal /run","description":"Register sprint-2 in the ledger with the 5 tasks, run via /run sprint-2 вҖ” implement + review-sprint + audit-sprint per task. Respects Loa CLAUDE.md to the letter. Tradeoff: heavyweight for pure FE viz polish; ~30-60% time overhead from gates that mostly check security/correctness which doesn't apply to canvas effects."},{"label":"Hybrid: ad-hoc commits, batch review at the end","description":"Iterate ad-hoc through all 5 changes, then run a single /review-sprint pass at the end before the demo recording. Catches obvious regressions without per-task overhead."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Should I run the dev server in the background so we can validate visually after each change?","header":"Dev server","options":[{"label":"Yes, start it now (Recommended)","description":"I run pnpm dev in the background; you watch http://localhost:3000/observatory in a browser and we validate each change visually before committing."},{"label":"No, I'll start it myself","description":"You start the dev server in your own terminal вҖ” I'll just edit code and you tell me what you see."},{"label":"Skip the dev server, just edit","description":"We trust types + the existing tests and ship the changes without visual validation in this session."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How do you want to handle the mass-conservation pulse?","header":"Pulse handling","options":[{"label":"Strip event pulses, keep weather equilibrium only (Recommended)","description":"Canvas keeps the slow weather-driven baseline (amplified element brighter, others slightly dimmer) but drops per-activity-event pulses entirely вҖ” no more ping rings, no more halo flash on each event. Cleanest separation: rail = per-event narrative; canvas = ambient world mood. The mass-conservation logic stays for the slow weather drift only."},{"label":"Keep structure, tone way down","description":"Mass-conservation logic, attention budget, and ping rings all stay вҖ” but radically attenuated. PULSE_BUMP cut to 0.3, ping rings shrunk to 60px max with 25% alpha and 1px stroke, halo response barely perceptible. The structural idea is still there if you watch for 30s."},{"label":"Revert entirely and move to change #3","description":"Roll back all the energy/ping-ring code I just added, return PentagramCanvas to its pre-change state (just weather coupling + sprite tides), and start the focus-card-on-sprite-click change next."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How do you want to unblock ActivityRail's KpiCell?","header":"KpiCell fix","options":[{"label":"I'll synthesize a minimal KpiCell matching the FE idiom (Recommended)","description":"I add a small inline component to ActivityRail.tsx following the existing pattern (glyph + big mono value + tiny uppercase label, no chrome) so the page renders. You can refine the styling later. Fastest unblock."},{"label":"Comment out the 3 KpiCell lines + their wrapper div","description":"Most conservative вҖ” page renders without the KPI mini-cells, leaving room for you to finish the component yourself. Zero guessing on my part."},{"label":"Paste your draft KpiCell here and I'll wire it in","description":"You have a half-finished component somewhere. Drop it in chat and I'll integrate it precisely as you intend."}],"multiSelect":false}]}
